title: 48/64 PASS: fundamental (d,e)=(2,1) — 48L=48, equilateral polynomial (4,4,4)
_fixture_purpose: Anchor fixture. Two faces of the same coin: (1) ALGEBRAIC: for direction (2,1), L=1, H=7, I=1: 48L=48=H²-I²=4CF — the minimum value. This is the origin-of-24 corollary: 2CF=24L (cert [137]) doubled gives H²-I²=4CF=48L. (2) POLYNOMIAL: the equilateral null triangle has biangle triple (P,R,T)=(4,4,4) satisfying PR+RT+PT=48, PRT=64 — the polynomial (x-4)³=x³-12x²+48x-64. This is the UNIQUE symmetric (P=R=T) solution. Connected to: 48=2×cosmos_period=2×24 (cert [128]: cosmos orbit has period 24); 64=satellite_period²=8² (cert [128]: satellite orbit has period 8); 48/64=3/4=equilateral spread in Wildberger chromogeometry.

equilateral_polynomial._note: The symmetric solution P=R=T=4 to (PR+RT+PT=48, PRT=64) represents the equilateral null triangle — three null biangles all equal to 4.

connections.cert_137: 48L=H²-I²=4CF ≥ 48 for all primitive directions (L≥1 since CF/12≥1); min at (2,1) with L=1
connections.cert_133: Eisenstein equilateral: three biangles P=R=T=4 at 120° spacing; (4)³=64 in Z[ω]
connections.cert_128: cosmos=24=period of spread polynomials in F₇; satellite=8; 48=2×24, 64=8²
